content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Andrew Morris 
Town Attorney 
Town of Truckee 
IO 183 Truckee Airport Road 
Truckee, CA 96161-3306 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-032 
Dear Mr. Morris: 
March 10, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Truckee Town Council member 
David Tirman regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the .. Act").1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section 1090. We are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby ( 1975) I 
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. 
QUESTION 
Understanding that Council member Tirman has a conflict of interest under the Act which 
prohibits his participation in governmental decisions related to his employer, JMA Ventures LLC 
("JMA"), what interactions may he have with Town staff in regards to the land use entitlements 
application by his employer? 
CONCLUSION 
Due to his financial interest in JMA Ventures as a source of income, Council member 
Tirman is prohibited from making, participating in making or influencing a decision relating to 
JMA Ventures. This prohibition includes contacting Town staff who are under the authority or 
budgetary control of the Town Council, for the purpose of affecting a decision. He may only 
contact staff for the limited purpose of submitting technical documents, or asking for factual 
information that is readily available to the general public, as discussed below. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission arc contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No. A-17-032 
Page No. 2 
Town Council member Tinnan is employed by JMA Ventures LLC, ("JMA") as a project 
manager. JMA is a real estate investment, development, and operations finn doing business 
primarily in Northern California. As project manager for JMA, Mr. Tinnan works to obtain land 
use entitlements from local governments. 
JMA has applied to the Town of Truckee ("the Town") for discretionary entitlements on real 
property, relating to infrastructure requirements, in an area referred to as "Planned Community 3" 
(''PC-3"). The Town previously adopted standards requiring certain infrastructure to be constructed 
as part of the subdivision and development of PC-3. These standards do not pennit parcels to be 
developed without construction of on- and off-site infrastructure, such as sidewalks and bicycle 
paths, occurring first. 
The Town Engineer has the discretion to phase infrastructure requirements based on the 
impacts associated with a specific development proposal. JMA would like to work with the Town 
Engineer in order to determine the phasing of specific infrastructure requirements for PC-3. In 
addition, JMA wishes to engage with Town staff regarding the process for obtaining the requested 
entitlements and details of the Town's requirements. In his employment as the project manager for 
JMA, Council member Tinnan would ordinarily handle such interactions and discussions on behalf 
of his employer. 
You have advised Council member Tinnan that he has a disqualifying financial interest 
under the Act due to his financial interest in JMA as a salaried employee. To date, he has avoided 
all contact with Town staff related to JMA and the PC-3 project. The Town staff, including the 
Town Engineer, are under the authority or budgetary control of the Town Council. You request 
specific advice as to the type of contacts between Council member Tinnan and Town staff that may 
be pennissible under the Act. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or 
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the 
official knows or has reason to know he or she has a financial interest. As you note, Council 
member Tinnan has a disqualifying interest in JMA as a source of income. (Section 87103(c) and 
Regulation 18702.3.) He will recuse himself from all Town Council decisions regarding JMA. 
(Regulation 18707.) 
Making, Participating in, and Influencing a Decision: 
A public official "makes a governmental decision" when the official does any of the 
following actions: authorizes or directs any action, votes, appoints a person, obligates or commits 
his or her agency to any course of action, or enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of his 
or her agency. (See Regulation 18704(a).) 


File No. A-17-032 
Page No. 3 
A public official "participates in making a governmental decision," when the official 
provides information, an opinion, or a recommendation for the purpose of affecting the decision 
without significant intervening substantive review. (See Regulation 18704(b).) 
A public official "uses his or her official position to influence a governmental decision" if 
he or she makes the following contacts or appearances: 
( 1) Contacts or appears before any official in his or her agency or in an agency 
subject to the authority or budgetary control of his or her agency for the purpose 
of affecting a decision; or 
(2) Contacts or appears before any official in any other government agency for the 
purpose of affecting a decision, and the public official acts or purports to act 
within his or her authority or on behalf of his or her agency in making the contact. 
(Regulation 18704(c).) 
Because Town staff is under the authority or the budgetary control of the Town 
Council, Mr. Tirman may not, for the purpose of affecting a decision, contact any Town 
staff on behalf of JMA's application. (Regulation 18704(c)(I). Specifically, he is 
prohibited from discussing or engaging with staff related to the JMA application. 
He may ask staff factual questions related to the JMA application, so long as the 
questions are general and relate to information available to the general public. We have 
previously advised that a council member may contact city staff to pose general questions 
(Torrance Advice Letter, No. A-94-084), and that the Act would not prohibit a public 
official "from approaching city staff to request city documents pertaining to the project 
that are otherwise available to the general public." (Bums Advice Letter, No. A-06-178.) 
General questions include whether a particular report has been completed or when a 
report will be available for public review. (Bums, supra.) 
Exceptions: 
Certain actions are exempted from the definition of "making, participating or influencing a 
government decision," and applicable to these facts, include the submission of technical documents 
as follows: 
(6) Architectural and Engineering Documents. 
(A) Drawings or submissions of an architectural, engineering, or similar nature 
prepared by a public official for a client to submit in a proceeding before the 
official's agency if: 
(i) The work is performed pursuant to the official's profession; and 


File No. A-17-032 
Page No. 4 
(ii) The official does not make any contact with the agency other than contact 
with agency staff concerning the process or evaluation of the documents prepared 
by the official. 
(Regulation 18704(d).) 
Under this exception, he may submit technical documents, so long as his contacts with staff 
in doing so do not relate to substantive issues, such as the contents or an evaluation of the 
documents. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
LKH:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
By: 
L. Karen Harrison 
Counsel, Legal Division

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or 
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the 
official knows or has reason to know he or she has a financial interest. As you note, Council

Because Town staff is under the authority or the budgetary control of the Town

Exceptions: 
Certain actions are exempted from the definition of "making, participating or influencing a 
government decision," and applicable to these facts, include the submission of technical documents 
as follows: 
(6) Architectural and Engineering Documents. 
(A) Drawings or submissions of an architectural, engineering, or similar nature 
prepared by a public official for a client to submit in a proceeding before the 
official's agency if:

with agency staff concerning the process or evaluation of the documents prepared

(Regulation 18704(d).) 
Under this exception, he may submit technical documents, so long as his contacts with staff

documents.
